uk: Неділя 27-ма по Зісланні Святого Духа. Глас 2. Єв. 5.
saints.en: 26th Sunday after Pentecost. St. Martyr Paramon. St. Martyr Philumenus. Ven. Acacius, mentioned in the Ladder

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» 10 стихир: 7 воскресних, 3 мчч., Сл., І нині: богородичний-догмат 2-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня. Стиховня неділ. По «Отче наш» тропар воскресний, Сл.: мчч., І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом тропаря мчч. Великий відпуст з помин. неділі і мчч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь» тропар воскресний (2), Сл.: мчч., І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом Сл. Катизми черг., сідал. неділ., полієлей, «Ангельський собор», іпакой, степенна та прокімен 2-го гласу. Єв. – Лк. 113 зач.; 24, 12-35. «Воскресіння Христове». Пс. 50. Стихира неділ. Канони воскресний, хрестовоскресний, Богородиці і мчч. Катавасія після кожної пісні – «Христос народжується». По 3-ій пісні – кондак-ікос і сідальний мчч. По 6-ій – кондак-ікос недільні. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – «Свят Господь Бог наш» і світильний воскресний, Сл.: І нині: богород. воскресного світильного. Хвалитних стихир – 8 воскресних, Сл.: стихира євангельська, І нині: «Преблагословенна». Після Вел. славослов'я – тропар «Воскресши з гробу». Відпуст великий з помин. служби воскресної і мчч.
Часи: на всіх – тропар воскресний, Слава: на 3-му і 9-му – тропар мчч., на 6-му – храму. Кондаки: на 1-му і 9-му – воскресний, на 3-му – мчч., на 6-му храму.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний, Сл.: кондак воскресний, І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом Сл. Прокімен і алилуя воскресні. Ряд.: Ап. – Еф. 233 зач.; 6, 10-17. Єв. – Лк. 71 зач.; 13, 10-17. Причасний «Хваліте».

troparion_en.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Acacius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

matched_oca_saint: Venerable Acacius of Sinai, who is mentioned in the Ladder

oca_saints.name: Martyr Paramon and 370 Martyrs in Bithynia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Paramon and Philoumenus and those with them, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: Urged on by the commandments of God, / with patience, you cleansed your souls from all defilement. / You reached perfection as spiritual athletes; / you renounced sacrifice to idols. / Imitating Christ, you were slain with a spear, / most blessed Paramon and Philoumenus. / Always fervently intercede with Christ on behalf of the world.

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Paramon and Philoumenus and those with them, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: Urged on by the commandments of God, / with patience, you cleansed your souls from all defilement. / You reached perfection as spiritual athletes; / you renounced sacrifice to idols. / Imitating Christ, you were slain with a spear, / most blessed Paramon and Philoumenus. / Always fervently intercede with Christ on behalf of the world.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Valerian, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Valerian;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Phaedrus, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Phaedrus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Acacius of Sinai, who is mentioned in the Ladder

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Acacius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Nectarius the Obedient of the Kiev Near Caves

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Father,  /  for you took up the Cross and followed after Christ. /  By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away  / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal.  /  Therefore, O venerable Nektarios, your spirit rejoices with the angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the Highest...”) Having divinely armed yourself with purity of soul and firmly grasping unceasing prayer as a
spear,  /  you have slain the armies of the demons.  /  Therefore, we beseech you, O Father Nektarios: /  
“Always intercede for those who honor you.”

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Abibus, Bishop of Nekresi in Georgia

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Abibus, entreat Christ God to save our souls.
